goal: Fix three coderef-scan resolver gaps that leave 75.9% of edges unresolved on Primary-Sources (13.3% resolution on resolvable edges): (1) add a Python stdlib builtin list so pathlib/json/re/csv/psycopg2 imports classify builtin/external not unresolved (~550 edges); (2) make the EXISTING loadTsconfigPaths actually resolve @/ alias imports (it's wired but missing PS's @/* patterns, ~400 edges); (3) populate receiverText + track receiver types so member/method calls (.join/.map, imported-symbol methods) resolve instead of callee_not_in_symbol_table (~15,269 unresolved calls). #1+#2 are cheap deterministic wins that should lift resolution past 50%; #3 is the large structural one.

tasks.description: Failing tests: resolver-python-stdlib.test.ts (import pathlib/json/re/psycopg2 from a .py -> external/builtin, not unresolved) + resolver-tsconfig-paths.test.ts (@/lib/x and @/components/ui/* resolve to local files given a tsconfig paths fixture)

Gap #1: add PYTHON_STDLIB set in import-resolver; bare Python imports matching it classify external reason=python_stdlib (graph-builder maps to builtin like node_builtin). Gap #2: debug + fix loadTsconfigPaths so @/* aliases resolve (baseUrl/glob handling)
Build; re-scan PS + coderef-core; record resolution-rate delta (target: past 50% on PS from #1+#2)

Failing test resolver-receiver-tracking.test.ts: member call obj.method() populates receiverText; imported-symbol method resolves; bare prototype method (.join/.map on unknown) carries probableBuiltinMember

Gap #3: populate receiverText on member-call RawCallFacts at extraction; resolve imported-symbol methods via the import binding; reclassify JS prototype-method calls (extend probableBuiltinMember path)
Build; re-scan PS + coderef-core; record final resolution-rate delta vs the 13.3% baseline
Commit P2 + push; CHANGELOG; roadmap update

plan_type_rationale: Three independent resolver fixes with measurable per-fix payoff; phase by gap so resolution-rate evidence is captured incrementally. P1 = cheap deterministic wins (#1+#2), P2 = structural receiver tracking (#3).
